day_trips_wine.name: Roero, Piedmont (Roero Arneis and Roero DOCG)
day_trips_wine.description: Across the Tanaro from Alba, the sandy Roero hills make delicate Arneis whites and lighter, perfumed Nebbiolo reds, with Canale and its Enoteca Regionale del Roero as the hub.
day_trips_wine.tip: Pair a Malvira Arneis tasting in Canale with the Enoteca Regionale; the Roero's sandy soils give a softer, earlier-drinking Nebbiolo than the Langhe.

day_trips_wine.name: Asti and Monferrato, Piedmont (Barbera, Ruche, Grignolino)
day_trips_wine.description: The rolling Monferrato around Asti is Barbera country, topped by Nizza DOCG, with curiosities like aromatic Ruche and pale, peppery Grignolino to discover.
day_trips_wine.tip: Base in Nizza Monferrato for the deepest Barbera; the Ruche of Castagnole Monferrato is the aromatic red almost no one outside Piedmont knows.

day_trips_wine.name: Gavi, Piedmont (Cortese di Gavi DOCG)
signature_pours: Gavi del Comune di Gavi DOCG
day_trips_wine.description: Toward the Ligurian border, the medieval town of Gavi gives its name to Piedmont's most famous dry white, crisp floral Cortese from houses like La Scolca and Villa Sparina.
day_trips_wine.tip: Book La Scolca at Rovereto di Gavi for the iconic black-label Gavi dei Gavi; the town pairs Cortese with the local raviole del plin and seafood.

day_trips_wine.name: Alta Langa, Piedmont (Alta Langa DOCG sparkling)
day_trips_wine.description: The high hills south of Alba grow Pinot Nero and Chardonnay for Alta Langa, Piedmont's traditional-method sparkling, in cooler, wilder country than the Barolo zone.
day_trips_wine.tip: Alta Langa is always vintage-dated and bottle-fermented; treat it as Piedmont's answer to Champagne and Franciacorta rather than to Prosecco.

day_trips_wine.name: Valle d'Aosta (Donnas mountain Nebbiolo)
day_trips_wine.description: Italy's smallest region grows high-altitude Nebbiolo, known locally as Picotendro, at Donnas near the Piedmont border, plus alpine natives like Petit Rouge and Fumin.
day_trips_wine.tip: Donnas Nebbiolo is leaner and more savoury than Langhe Barolo; it makes the long drive a study in how altitude reshapes the same grape.

day_trips_wine.name: Oltrepo Pavese, Lombardy (Pinot Nero and Metodo Classico)
day_trips_wine.description: Over the Lombardy border south of Pavia, the Oltrepo Pavese is a major Pinot Nero zone, making still reds and traditional-method sparkling around Casteggio.
day_trips_wine.tip: Come for the Metodo Classico sparkling from Pinot Nero; Casteggio sits twenty minutes off the motorway and pairs well with a Gavi stop on the way.

day_trips_wine.name: Dogliani, Piedmont (Dolcetto di Dogliani DOCG)
day_trips_wine.description: South of Barolo, Dogliani is the spiritual home of Dolcetto, with its own DOCG for a deeper, more structured wine than everyday Alba Dolcetto.
day_trips_wine.tip: Dogliani Dolcetto can age a few years, unlike most Dolcetto; it is the best-value serious red in the southern Langhe.
